axis: bridge / alert subscriber — JSONL sibling persistence + per-rule cooldown dedup
title: atlas_alert_subscriber — JSONL sibling history (bridge-23) + per-rule cooldown dedup (bridge-24)
summary: Combined cycle for two orthogonal-but-same-file features in tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa. PART A (ω-bridge-23): in addition to bridge-21's TSV append, mirror every FIRED event into atlas_alert_history.jsonl as a JSON-Lines row with scope='atlas_alert' (discriminator vs scope='bridge_anomaly' / 'bridge_registry'). Independent failure handling — JSONL fault doesn't invalidate TSV success. PART B (ω-bridge-24): per-rule cooldown — same rule firing within --cooldown-minutes M of the LAST same-rule TSV row is SUPPRESSED, NOT persisted to TSV/JSONL, but emitted as a distinct __ATLAS_ALERT__ SUPPRESSED sentinel so operators can distinguish dedup from quiescence. Both features off-by-default for full backward compat with bridge-21 callers.

predecessors.omega_bridge_17: atlas_alert_subscriber initial — JSONL stream alerter on bridge_anomaly rows
predecessors.omega_bridge_21: TSV history persistence at state/atlas_alert_history.tsv (raw 77, FIRED only). This cycle adds the JSONL sibling and the cooldown gate around the FIRED-emit path.

context.problem_part_a: TSV is grep-friendly but downstream tooling (jq pipelines, alerting integrations, schema-evolution-tolerant consumers) prefers JSON-Lines. bridge-16 established this dual-write pattern for bridge_anomaly; bridge-23 mirrors it for atlas_alert.
context.problem_part_b: Today the same rule can re-fire repeatedly within seconds while underlying conditions persist. Floods both stdout and the history TSV. Operators want a cooldown gate so a rule that fired N minutes ago doesn't re-fire if the same N+δ check produces the same verdict.

site_a_jsonl_sibling: JSON body builders per rule (warn_ratio / insufficient_ratio / consecutive_warn / recent_warn_ratio)
history-append block emits both TSV and JSONL with INDEPENDENT try/catch failure handling — TSV success not invalidated by JSONL fault and vice versa
site_b_cooldown: DEDUP_TAIL_K=20 perf bound — only scan last 20 TSV rows for same-rule history

post-rule-firing dedup block: tail TSV → strip header → for each candidate rule, walk lines reverse, find last same-rule ts, compute (now - ts) in minutes via python3 datetime.strptime, compare to cooldown_min
suppress drops the candidate from fired/event_rows/event_jsonl_bodies/fired_rules and pushes a distinct __ATLAS_ALERT__ SUPPRESSED sentinel
raw 71 honoured: SUPPRESSED is fully ephemeral — no TSV/JSONL row written

jsonl_row.ts: system-clock UTC at emit time (matches sibling TSV ts exactly — both written from the same now_iso variable)
jsonl_row.scope: atlas_alert (constant; discriminator vs other JSONL emitters)
jsonl_row.rule: one of warn_ratio | insufficient_ratio | consecutive_warn | recent_warn_ratio
jsonl_row.ratio: float — observed ratio (0.0 for streak rule which has no ratio)
jsonl_row.threshold: configured threshold (0 for streak which uses K instead)
jsonl_row.last_n: configured last-N window (0 for recent_warn_ratio which uses minutes)
jsonl_row.extra: rule-specific encoded string ('' for ratio rules, 'streak=K' for consecutive_warn, 'window_minutes=M' for recent_warn_ratio)

validation.step: fixture A — FIRE → TSV + JSONL both get rows
validation.result: TSV: 1 row warn_ratio; JSONL: 1 row scope=atlas_alert with all 8 fields, parses cleanly through python -m json.tool

validation.step: fixture B — cooldown=10, retry within window
validation.command: second invocation against same fixture with --cooldown-minutes 10
validation.result: __ATLAS_ALERT__ SUPPRESSED rule=warn_ratio reason=cooldown last_fired_ago_min=0.2365 cooldown_minutes=10; TSV row count stays at 1; JSONL row count stays at 1 — suppression IS ephemeral as designed
FIRED normally, identical behaviour to bridge-21

both warn_ratio and consecutive_warn FIRED in same invocation; TSV gets 2 rows; JSONL gets 2 rows; row order matches firing order; consecutive_warn JSONL row correctly encodes ratio=0.0, threshold=0, extra=streak=2

validation.step: fixture E — cooldown active but TSV missing
validation.result: first FIRED emits normally — no prior to compare against

validation_caveats.remote_routing_interference: Same as ω-cleanup-1: /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/hexa wrapper routes through hexa_remote when hetzner is reachable, hanging python3 sub-execs in the test path. Workaround: invoke /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp directly.

scope_constraints_honoured: raw 71 report-only: SUPPRESSED is ephemeral, never persisted; HISTORY_WRITE_FAIL is non-fatal sentinel
raw 73 admissibility: --cooldown-minutes / ATLAS_ALERT_COOLDOWN / ATLAS_ALERT_HISTORY_JSONL all scope-isolated
raw 77 append-only respect: TSV unchanged write semantics; new JSONL is append-only by construction (one object per line)
raw 80 sentinel: existing FIRED/PASS/SKIP shapes unchanged; new SUPPRESSED is additive
no parallel-session collision: only tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa edited; cli/run.hexa, atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, n6/*, kick_*, design/kick/*, .own all untouched
no commit in anima — closure JSON written as untracked
agent_handoff_note: ω-bridge-23+24 was launched as a background agent that completed the .hexa edit (+172/-9) but did not commit or write closure JSON before the session terminated. Main session validated all 5 fixtures + closed the cycle directly. Closure + commit are MAIN-SESSION work; .hexa edits are AGENT-AUTHORED.
